---
title: "Intermediate_Elective_2"
author: "Alex Gonzales"
date: "5/15/26"
format: 
 pdf:
    include-in-header: 
       text: |
         \usepackage{fvextra}
         \DefineVerbatimEnvironment{Highlighting}{Verbatim}{breaklines,commandchars=\\\{\}}
         \DefineVerbatimEnvironment{OutputCode}{Verbatim}{breaklines,commandchars=\\\{\}}
execute: 
  message: false
  warning: false
---

# 1. Set up

## Packages
```{r}
#| label: packages
#| warning: false
#| message: false      
#| results: "hide"     


# general use and cleaning
library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(janitor)
library(snakecase)
library(NatParksPalettes)
library(RColorBrewer)

# reading .xlxs files
library(readxl)

# wrap axis labels
library(scales)

# visualize missing data
library(naniar)

# arrange plots
library(patchwork)
```


## Data


```{r}
#| label: data

# read in vegatation data
veg <- read_csv(here("data", "veg.csv"))

# vegetation survey metadata
metadata <- read_csv(here("data", "vp_veg_metadata.csv"))

# taxonomic information
taxon_list <- read_csv(here("data", "taxon_list.csv"))

aq_ins <- read_xlsx(
  here("data", "Aquatic Sampling Data-2026-03-10.xlsx"), 
  sheet = "Aquatic Insects")


# site information
sites <- read_xlsx(
  here("data", "Aquatic Sampling Data-2026-03-10.xlsx"), 
  sheet = "sites")

# water quality
water_quality <- read_xlsx(
  here("data", "Aquatic Sampling Data-2026-03-10.xlsx"), 
  sheet = "Water Quality",
  # powerful argument in read_xlsx to show the missing variables as numbers
  na = c("", "n/a", "N/A", "over", "173+"))

```



# Problems

## 1. Explain your inspiration

I chose the "Coal falls. Renewables rise" visualization because I want to compare the percent cover of native plants over non-native plants through time. Therefore my variables are percent cover, and time, with two different line plots: Native and Non-Native cover.

```{r}
#| label: class-code
#| message: false

# created a new object from veg
pools_explicit0_25 <- veg |> 
  # filtered to only include vernal pool transects
  filter(str_detect(transect_name, "VP") &
           site == "ncos") |> 
  # added new rows to dataframe for missing species and transect distances for each year and pool
  complete(year, transect_name, psoc, transect_distance,
           # filled missing percent cover values with 0
           fill = list(percent_cover = 0)) |> 
  # grouped by year, pool, species, and cover category
  group_by(year, transect_name, psoc, cover_category) |> 
  # summarized by calculating total percent cover across all quadrats
  summarize(sum_pc = sum(percent_cover, na.rm = TRUE)) |> 
  # ungrouped the data fram
  ungroup() |> 
  # put remove = TRUE to remove year and transect name columns to avoid redundancy
  # created a column to join with metadata
  unite("year_pool", year, transect_name, remove = TRUE) |> 
  # joined metadata data frame
  left_join(metadata, by = "year_pool") |> 
  # calculated mean percent cover
  mutate(mean_pc = sum_pc/num_quad) |> 
  # only include 2025 data
  filter(year == "2025")


total_pool_coverage <- pools_explicit0_25 %>%
  group_by(psoc, cover_category) %>%
  summarize(
    n = sum(mean_pc, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()
```

```{r}
#| label: wrangling-data

dt <- total_pool_coverage |> 
  rename(designer = psoc,
    category = cover_category) |> 
  arrange(desc(n)) |> 
  filter(n > 5) |> 
  mutate(n = sqrt(n),
  designer = factor(designer, levels = designer))


dt <- mutate_at(dt, vars(category), as.factor)
dt <- mutate_at(dt, vars(designer), as.factor)
glimpse(dt)

```

```{r}
#| label: prepping-plot

# Start by setting the plot 
# Set a number of 'empty bar' to add at the end of each group
empty_bar <- 2
# Add lines to the initial dataset
to_add <- matrix(NA, empty_bar, ncol(dt))
colnames(to_add) <- colnames(dt)
dt <- rbind(dt, to_add)
dt$id <- seq(1, nrow(dt))

# Get the name and the y position of each label
label_data <- dt
number_of_bar <- nrow(label_data)
angle <- 90 - 360 * (label_data$id-0.5) /number_of_bar     
label_data$hjust <- ifelse(angle < -90, 1, 0)
label_data$angle <- ifelse(angle < -90, angle+180, angle)

# palette
pal <- brewer.pal(11, "Spectral")
```

```{r}
#| label: visualizing-w-plot

ggplot(dt) +
  geom_bar(aes(x = as.factor(designer), y = n, fill = category), stat = "identity") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = pal,
    name = "Category",
    labels = c("Bare Ground", "Native Cover", "Non-Native Cover", "Other Cover", "Thatch")
  ) +
  ylim(-8, 25) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.margin = unit(rep(-1, 4), "cm"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10)
  ) +
  labs(title = "Species Cover across Vernal Pools 2025") +
  coord_polar(start = 0) +
  geom_text(data = label_data,
  aes(x = as.factor(designer),
      y = max(dt$n, na.rm = TRUE) + 0.3,   # <-- reduced from 1.5
      label = str_replace_all(designer, "_", " "),  # <-- removes underscores
      hjust = hjust),
  color = "black",
  fontface = "bold",
  alpha = 0.6,
  size = 3.0,
  angle = label_data$angle,
  inherit.aes = FALSE
)

```


```{r}
# wrangling
dt <- total_pool_coverage |> 
  rename(designer = psoc, category = cover_category) |> 
  arrange(desc(n)) |>
  filter(n > 5) |>
  mutate(n = sqrt(n))

# empty bars
empty_bar <- 2
to_add <- matrix(NA, empty_bar, ncol(dt))
colnames(to_add) <- colnames(dt)
dt <- rbind(dt, to_add)
dt$id <- seq(1, nrow(dt))

# label data — use id just like the original
label_data <- dt
number_of_bar <- nrow(label_data)
angle <- 90 - 360 * (label_data$id - 0.5) / number_of_bar
label_data$hjust <- ifelse(angle < -90, 1, 0)
label_data$angle <- ifelse(angle < -90, angle + 180, angle)

# plot
ggplot(dt) +
  geom_bar(aes(x = as.factor(id), y = n, fill = category), stat = "identity") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = pal,
    name = "Category",
    labels = c("Bare Ground", "Native Cover", "Non-Native Cover", "Other Cover", "Thatch"),
    na.translate = FALSE
  ) +
  ylim(-8, 25) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.margin = unit(rep(-1, 4), "cm")
  ) +
  labs(title = "Species Cover across Vernal Pools 2025") +
  coord_polar(start = 0) +
  geom_text(
    data = label_data,
    aes(x = id,
        y = n + 1,
        label = str_replace_all(designer, "_", " "),
        hjust = hjust),
    color = "black",
    fontface = "bold",
    alpha = 0.6,
    size = 3.0,
    angle = label_data$angle,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  )
```
